notes: v1.14.116 - B1 HOTFIX (UI walkthrough, BUGS list): New-Deployment scope picker (Work>Deploy>+NEW) - cabinet selection wouldn't register, STAGE SCOPE SNAPSHOT stayed disabled. ROOT CAUSE (browser-verified): the Cancel/STAGE action bar (.msc-actions, position:sticky;bottom:0;z-index:5) was clamped to #pg-work's content-box bottom, because in the redesign #pg-work is the scroll container and carries a large padding-bottom to clear the fixed #rd-botnav. So the bar floated ~mid-viewport OVER the cab rows and intercepted their taps (elementFromPoint returned the bar, not the row) - selection never incremented, button never enabled. position:fixed is no escape (#wk-deploy has an identity transform from its rf-fade animation, trapping fixed to the scroll content). FIX (redesign-only, body.rd-gated): while the scope picker is shown, shrink #pg-work's bottom padding to just the nav height via `body.rd #pg-work:has(.msc-actions){padding-bottom:calc(71px+var(--safe-bottom))!important}` so the sticky bar pins ABOVE the nav instead of over the list; foot-pad runway bumped to 88px so the last row clears it. The base .msc-actions/.msc-foot-pad rules are UNCHANGED - legacy (?legacy=1, #ops-content host, no padding clamp) is byte-identical. Same root cause as the walkthrough's Rule D drift (action bars pin to bottom) - the broader pin-everywhere sweep is still a separate later pass. Verified via agent-browser at 412x915: bar pins flush above nav at top+bottom scroll, zero rows covered, 9 real taps -> 9 selected -> button enabled. No JS/markup change; CSS-only. DEVICE-VERIFY: Work>Deploy>+NEW, load a Master, tap cabinets -> count climbs + STAGE enables; Cancel/STAGE bar sits above the bottom nav, never floats over rows; ?legacy=1 picker unchanged.
